# Portfolio Tracker Website

## Scope of the project

You must build a web application that allows users to track their portfolios. The application should allow users to create an account, add their investments, and view their portfolio performance over time. The application should also provide the ability to upload an xlsx file with the user's investments, and the application should parse the file and add the investments to the user's portfolio. Purpose of the application is to show the user how their portfolio is performing over time, and to provide insights into their investments. It must calculate yield daily, weekly, monthly, year to date, yearly, 3 years, 5 years and all time. It should also show the user how their portfolio is performing compared to the S&P 500 index. The application should be built using React for the frontend and Node.js for the backend. The application should use a database to store user information and investment data. It will be a No-Sql database because data must be flexible and scalable. The application should be deployed to a cloud platform such as Azure.

## Plan of the development

### Phase 1: Analyze the xlsx file format, analyze what info do you have in this file

Parse the file for extracting the following information: investment name, ticker symbol, ISIN, category of the investment (Stock, Bond, Mutual Fund, ETF, Commodities), number of shares, purchase price, purchase date, sold date (if applicable), sale price (if applicable). This information will be used to calculate the performance of the user's portfolio over time. The application should be able to handle missing or incomplete data, and should provide feedback to the user if there are any issues with the file they uploaded. Data uploaded must be idempotent so user can upload this file multiple times without creating duplicate entries in the database. The application should also provide the ability to edit or delete investments after they have been added to the portfolio.

### Phase 2: Design the database schema to store user information and investment data

Transform the extracted information from the xlsx file into a format that can be stored in a No-Sql database. The database schema should be designed to allow for flexible and scalable storage of user information and investment data. The schema should include collections for users, investments, and portfolio performance data.

### Phase 3: Identify a strategy for getting history data for the investments as stocks, bonds and exchange ratios 

The application should be able to retrieve historical data for the investments in the user's portfolio. This data will be used to calculate the performance of the portfolio over time. The application should use a third-party API to retrieve this data, and should be able to handle any issues with the API such as rate limits or downtime. The application should also provide the ability to manually upload historical data for investments if the user prefers to do so.

### Phase 4: Implement the backend using Node.js

The backend of the application should be implemented using Node.js. The backend should provide APIs for user authentication, investment management, and portfolio performance calculation. The backend should also handle the parsing of the xlsx file and the storage of user information and investment data in the database. The backend should be designed to be scalable and secure, and should follow best practices for API development.

### Phase 5: Implement the frontend using React

The frontend of the application should be implemented using React. The frontend should provide a user-friendly interface for users to interact with the application. The frontend should allow users to create an account, add their investments, view their portfolio performance over time, and compare their performance to the S&P 500 index. The frontend should also provide the ability to upload an xlsx file with the user's investments, and should provide feedback to the user if there are any issues with the file they uploaded. The frontend should be designed to be responsive and accessible, and should follow best practices for React development. The frontend must be optimized for desktop and mobile devices.

### Phase 6: Implement the calculation of portfolio performance

The application should calculate the performance of the user's portfolio over time. The performance should be calculated daily, weekly, monthly, year to date, yearly, 3 years, 5 years and all time. The application should also calculate the performance of the user's portfolio compared to the S&P 500 index. The performance calculations should be accurate and should take into account any dividends or other income generated by the investments in the portfolio. 
The performance must show graphical data to the user as line graph and heatmap graph. The graphs must shows the performance of single investment or entire portfolio over time selecting the time range. The graphs must also show the performance of the user's portfolio compared to the S&P 500 index.

### Phase 7: Deploy the application to a cloud platform

The application should be deployed to a cloud platform such as Azure. The deployment should be designed to be scalable and secure, and should follow best practices for cloud deployment. The application should be monitored for performance and security issues, and should be updated regularly to ensure that it remains secure and up-to-date with the latest technologies. Generate deploy scripts for the application to automate the deployment process. Choose your favorite azure resources for hosting the application, such as Azure App Service for the backend and Azure Static Web Apps for the frontend. Use Azure Cosmos DB for the No-Sql database to store user information and investment data. Select economic SKUs for the resources to minimize costs while ensuring good performance. Also implement a way to launch and test the application locally.
